name: As Mice by the Moorish Cottage – Salas Dam
name_original: As Maus por la Casiña da Moura – Presa de Salas
region_intro: Hiking around Muíños offers diverse trails through a landscape characterized by rolling hills, river valleys, and historical sites. The region, located in Galicia, Spain, features a mix of forested areas and open terrain, often with views of reservoirs and traditional architecture. Elevation changes are generally moderate, providing accessible routes for various fitness levels. The presence of ancient structures like dolmens adds a cultural dimension to the natural surroundings. Best hiking trails around Muíños The most popular hiking route is As Mice by the Moorish Cottage – Salas Dam, a 8.7 miles (13.9 km) trail that takes 3 hours 50 minutes to complete. This moderate route features views of the Salas Dam and passes by a historic cottage. Another top favourite among local hikers is View of Pitões Waterfall – Campesinho Stream loop from Pitões das Junias, a moderate 9.4 miles (15.1 km) path. This trail explores the area around Pitões das Junias, offering views of a waterfall and following the Campesinho Stream. Local hikers also love the Dolmen Casota do Foxo – Dolmen of Casola do Foxo loop from As Maus, a 7.9 miles (12.8 km) trail leading through varied terrain and past ancient dolmens, often completed in about 3 hours 25 minutes. Hiking around Muíños is defined by its river valleys, forested hills, and historical landmarks such as dolmens and old mills. The network of trails offers options for easy family friendly hikes as well as more advanced hiking trails. The routes in Muíños are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 70 reviews. More than 350 hikers have used komoot to explore Muíños's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are there in Muíños?
A: Muíños offers a diverse selection of over 20 hiking trails, ranging from easy strolls to more challenging routes. This variety ensures there's something for every fitness level and preference.

Q: Are there easy hiking trails suitable for beginners or families in Muíños?
A: Yes, Muíños has several easy hiking options perfect for beginners or families. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10459940" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Parking lot – View of Pitões Waterfall loop from Pitões das Junias&lt;/a&gt; is an easy 4.8 km route that offers beautiful scenery without significant elevation gain.

Q: Are there any hikes that feature waterfalls in Muíños?
A: Absolutely. The region is home to beautiful waterfalls. You can explore trails that lead to them, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/19879622" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of Pitões Waterfall – Campesinho Stream loop from Pitões das Junias&lt;/a&gt;, which offers views of the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6066651" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pitões das Júnias Waterfall&lt;/a&gt;. Another notable waterfall is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3622322" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Corgo da Fecha Waterfall&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What historical or natural landmarks can I see while hiking in Muíños?
A: Muíños is rich in both historical and natural landmarks. You can encounter ancient structures like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2479245" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Dolmen of Casola do Foxo&lt;/a&gt;, which is part of the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/5328420" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Dolmen Casota do Foxo – Dolmen of Casola do Foxo loop from As Maus&lt;/a&gt; trail. Other significant sites include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3112491" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Aquis Querquennis Roman Camp&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5278288" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Roman Baths of Bande&lt;/a&gt;. For natural wonders, don't miss the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5337265" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Río Caldo Hot Springs&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there circular hiking routes available in Muíños?
A: Yes, many of the trails in Muíños are designed as circular routes, allowing you to start and end at the same point without retracing your steps. Examples include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14935272" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hiking loop from Pitões das Junias&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e979343547/as-mice-by-the-moorish-cottage-salas-dam" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;As Mice by the Moorish Cottage – Salas Dam&lt;/a&gt; route.

Q: Can I bring my dog on hikes in Muíños?
A: Generally, dogs are welcome on most hiking trails in Muíños, especially in natural areas. However, it's always advisable to keep them on a leash, especially near livestock or in protected areas, and to clean up after them. Check local signage for any specific restrictions.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Muíños?
A: The best time for hiking in Muíños is typically during spring (April to June) and autumn (September to October). During these seasons, the weather is mild, and the landscapes are particularly vibrant, with blooming flowers in spring and rich colors in autumn. Summer can be warm, while winter offers a quieter experience, though some trails might be wet.

Q: Are there challenging hikes for experienced hikers in Muíños?
A: While most trails in Muíños are moderate, there are options for experienced hikers seeking a challenge. The region features routes with more significant elevation changes and longer distances. There is at least one difficult trail available, offering a more strenuous experience for those looking for it.

Q: Is parking available at the trailheads in Muíños?
A: Parking is generally available at or near the main trailheads and popular starting points for hikes in Muíños. Many routes, especially those starting from villages or specific attractions, will have designated parking areas. It's always a good idea to check the specific route details on komoot for parking information.

Q: Do I need any permits to hike in Muíños?
A: For general hiking on established trails in Muíños, permits are typically not required. The region's trails are largely accessible to the public. However, if you plan to visit specific protected natural parks or engage in certain activities, it's always wise to check for any local regulations or requirements beforehand.

Q: What do other hikers say about the trails in Muíños?
A: The trails in Muíños are highly regarded by the komoot community, with an average rating of 4.4 stars from over 70 reviews. Hikers often praise the region's diverse landscapes, the presence of historical sites like dolmens, and the well-maintained paths that wind through river valleys and forested hills.

Q: Are there opportunities for wild swimming near the hiking trails in Muíños?
A: Yes, the region offers natural swimming spots. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5337265" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Río Caldo Hot Springs&lt;/a&gt; are a notable attraction where you can enjoy natural thermal waters, often accessible via nearby hiking routes. Always exercise caution and check local conditions before swimming in natural bodies of water.
tips: On a small hill from where you can see a good panoramic view of the As Maus de Salas Reservoir, we find the Casota do Foxo Dolmen. This monolithic complex was built between the 4th and 3rd centuries BC by Neolithic herdsmen.
The walkway over the Salas dam, in the Gerês-Xurés Transboundary Biosphere Reserve, offers beautiful views of the reservoir and the surrounding plain.
